title: claude-runner.md: planning an epic creates meta-work ticks (final review + close-out)
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §3 'Meta-work as ticks'. File: skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md only.

Add guidance (a subsection near 'The loop' or where epic planning is discussed) that when the orchestrator fleshes an epic into ticks it ALSO creates the process steps as ticks:

Epic X
├── <implementation ticks, waves as usual>
├── 'Final review of epic X diff'            --blocked-by <every last-wave implementation tick>
└── 'Close out epic X: retro + plan <next>'  --blocked-by <final review tick>

- The final-review tick's work: review the epic's full diff (dispatch a reviewer subagent for substantial epics, per the existing 'Reviewing the work' section) and fix or route findings before close-out.
- The close-out tick already exists as a convention in SKILL.md's Roadmaps section — reference it, don't redefine it. What is NEW here: the final-review step becomes a tick too, and both meta-ticks are created AT PLANNING TIME so the whole process is resumable from tick state alone.
- Update the numbered loop pseudocode: the 'Run a final review over the epic, then close it' step should note these are now ticks the loop reaches via tk next, not ad-hoc steps.
- One sentence on why: with meta-work as ticks, every tk next result maps to exactly one action, so a dead session resumes by re-invoking the skill (the full resume procedure is a separate tick — do not write it).

Do NOT touch SKILL.md or tick-patterns.md (another tick owns SKILL.md changes).
acceptance_criteria: claude-runner.md documents both meta-ticks with their blocker wiring, created at planning time; loop pseudocode references them; no duplication of SKILL.md's close-out definition
closed_reason: Meta-work ticks subsection merged into claude-runner.md (agent DONE)
